reason: A very sad event in real life and a dramatic genre or play with a sad ending are different referents, not two phrasings of one learner meaning.
semantic_distinction: sem_7c849edf4ec40cc136607c2e is grounded by the exact EN sense "very sad event" and learner example "It's a tragedy that she died so young.". sem_53e639095183a99335272c63 is grounded by the exact EN sense "serious sad play" and learner example "Shakespeare’s tragedies". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_7c849edf4ec40cc136607c2e names a real sad event or situation, often involving death; sem_53e639095183a99335272c63 names a serious dramatic work or genre with a sad ending.

reason: Exact or accurate manner modifies an event, while standalone 'precisely' is a discourse marker that affirms or emphasizes a prior statement; the grammatical functions are independently learnable.
semantic_distinction: sem_c9d5dc63d33d2add7912b2ee is grounded by the exact EN sense "exactly/accurately" and learner example "They look precisely the same to me.". sem_0c1919b3e0a80c40cdecebb5 is grounded by the exact EN sense "used for emphasis/agreement" and learner example "‘It's not that easy, is it?’ ‘No, precisely.’". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_c9d5dc63d33d2add7912b2ee functions as a manner adverb meaning exactly or accurately; sem_0c1919b3e0a80c40cdecebb5 functions as an emphasis or agreement marker responding to a proposition.

reason: A hypothesized future situation and a written outline of a film or play are different referents and domains despite sharing the Vietnamese word 'kịch bản'.
semantic_distinction: sem_02c07606dc4abb3d2d5e4fee is grounded by the exact EN sense "possible future situation" and learner example "Let me suggest a possible scenario.". sem_09d0ff377ae5694fa18f067d is grounded by the exact EN sense "film/play outline" and learner example "a film scenario". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_02c07606dc4abb3d2d5e4fee describes a possible sequence of future events used for planning; sem_09d0ff377ae5694fa18f067d is a written plot outline for a film or stage play.

reason: A crossed-line pattern, a lettered or numbered map reference system, and an electricity-distribution network are distinct concrete systems, not interchangeable instances of one definition.
semantic_distinction: sem_160f06aef688a3a6164fafc0 is grounded by the exact EN sense "line pattern" and learner example "New York’s grid of streets". sem_8409696d03b7fbbb28cfe857 is grounded by the exact EN sense "map squares" and learner example "The grid reference is C8.". sem_ca23049a29586a9792ec3e60 is grounded by the exact EN sense "power network" and learner example "the national grid (= the electricity supply in a country)". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_160f06aef688a3a6164fafc0 is a physical or visual pattern of crossing lines; sem_8409696d03b7fbbb28cfe857 is a coordinate framework of labelled map squares; sem_ca23049a29586a9792ec3e60 is a network that distributes electrical power.

reason: Physical or practical accessibility and intellectual comprehensibility impose different conditions and select different subjects and examples.
semantic_distinction: sem_a4b307b3f755cd9e6063e131 is grounded by the exact EN sense "easy to reach, enter, or use, including for people with disabilities" and learner example "The remote desert area is accessible only by helicopter.". sem_3de2e2e95cc141d77966911d is grounded by the exact EN sense "easy to understand" and learner example "Her poetry is always very accessible.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_a4b307b3f755cd9e6063e131 means easy to reach, enter, or use, including disability access; sem_3de2e2e95cc141d77966911d means easy to understand, as with poetry or ideas.

reason: All three uses involve pressure, but the learner-relevant result differs: deformation or injury, grinding into particles, or forced crowding into a confined space.
semantic_distinction: sem_bba6398d9097be184b6d467f is grounded by the exact EN sense "damage or injure by pressing very hard" and learner example "The car was completely crushed under the truck.". sem_6008df4e2e1ef7328fb36f84 is grounded by the exact EN sense "break into small pieces or powder by pressing" and learner example "Crush the biscuits finely before adding them to the mixture.". sem_6b37973b770f56b05bc2febe is grounded by the exact EN sense "push or press into a small space" and learner example "Over twenty prisoners were crushed into a small dark cell.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_bba6398d9097be184b6d467f is damage or injury caused by heavy pressure; sem_6008df4e2e1ef7328fb36f84 is reduction to small pieces or powder; sem_6b37973b770f56b05bc2febe is forcing people or things into a small space.

reason: A share taken from one whole and a ratio or relationship between two quantities answer different questions even though Vietnamese can use 'tỷ lệ' for both.
semantic_distinction: sem_85140e0ab8647436e74cf742 is grounded by the exact EN sense "part of a whole" and learner example "Water covers a large proportion of the earth's surface.". sem_38fd8bf70a4401181d9430bd is grounded by the exact EN sense "size/amount relationship" and learner example "The proportion (ratio) of men to women in the college has changed dramatically over the years.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_85140e0ab8647436e74cf742 denotes the part or share contributed by one subset of a whole; sem_38fd8bf70a4401181d9430bd denotes the comparative relationship in size or amount between two things.

reason: The related forms stay separate because combining them would require a machine-shaped adjective/noun gloss and would hide three different constructions: potential + noun, potential for, and possessive/full potential.
semantic_distinction: sem_2775cc572fedf4c370209f27 is grounded by the exact EN sense "able to develop / happen under the right conditions" and learner example "potential customers/buyers/investors/clients". sem_b5ee20d0651aa3cca7f91181 is grounded by the exact EN sense "the possibility of something happening, developing, or being used" and learner example "the potential for change". sem_1c84e62177cc7ca04af5dc35 is grounded by the exact EN sense "qualities or abilities that can be developed for future success" and learner example "All children should be encouraged to realize their full potential.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_2775cc572fedf4c370209f27 is the adjective for a person, thing, or event that may develop or happen; sem_b5ee20d0651aa3cca7f91181 is the noun possibility of an event, development, or use; sem_1c84e62177cc7ca04af5dc35 is undeveloped ability or qualities for future success.

reason: A physical part crossed at a doorway and an abstract point at which a condition begins are independently useful learner meanings; the shared Vietnamese root does not make them interchangeable.
semantic_distinction: sem_5524351aa292fd61ae7b47bc is grounded by the exact EN sense "doorway entrance" and learner example "He stepped across the threshold.". sem_b29ba53857725784af2b1833 is grounded by the exact EN sense "starting level / point" and learner example "He has a low boredom threshold (= he gets bored easily).". Reviewed learner contrast: One sense is the physical strip or entrance crossed at a doorway; the other is an abstract level or point at which a state begins.

reason: The noun names a disagreement, one verb challenges truth or legal validity, and the other verb argues over a contested matter such as a bill or territory.
semantic_distinction: sem_b37a43060b1c0c1189b9cb99 is grounded by the exact EN sense "argument" and learner example "industrial/pay disputes". sem_3037f3ff23ceea78f9658cb7 is grounded by the exact EN sense "question whether something is true or officially valid" and learner example "These figures have been disputed.". sem_cf863c65da15c9d78bfd9603 is grounded by the exact EN sense "argue strongly about something, especially ownership" and learner example "I tried to dispute the bill with the waiter.". Reviewed learner contrast: The noun is an argument as an event or state; the first verb questions truth or official validity; the second verb contends strongly about a matter, especially ownership.

reason: The ceremonial headpiece, the royal government as a legal authority, and the office or power of a monarch are three distinct metonymic referents.
semantic_distinction: sem_b9fe861503429e13933a193a is grounded by the exact EN sense "a ceremonial headpiece worn by a monarch" and learner example "The crown was placed upon the new monarch's head.". sem_00db6aa138ec9f1210422506 is grounded by the exact EN sense "government of a country represented by a king / queen" and learner example "land owned by the Crown". sem_f6c15217c4fe59e7fa943a1f is grounded by the exact EN sense "position / power of a king / queen" and learner example "She refused the crown.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_b9fe861503429e13933a193a is the physical headpiece worn by a monarch; sem_00db6aa138ec9f1210422506 is the state or government represented by a monarch; sem_f6c15217c4fe59e7fa943a1f is the office, position, or sovereign power of a monarch.

reason: Geometric equidistance, metaphorical similarity or simultaneous development, and a noun naming an analogous instance differ in relational constraint and grammatical role.
semantic_distinction: sem_d7a2da94788ea6c9cb8d5ee8 is grounded by the exact EN sense "same distance apart" and learner example "parallel lines". sem_0f7b167bb80b4f59dea7fabb is grounded by the exact EN sense "very similar / happening together" and learner example "parallel trends". sem_431969c49908d5f75a4e70af is grounded by the exact EN sense "similar thing" and learner example "These ideas have parallels (equivalent) in Freud's thought too.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_d7a2da94788ea6c9cb8d5ee8 is an adjective requiring lines or paths to remain the same distance apart; sem_0f7b167bb80b4f59dea7fabb is an adjective for similar or concurrent developments; sem_431969c49908d5f75a4e70af is a noun naming a comparable counterpart or analogy.

reason: Recognizing or showing a difference is a comparison operation, whereas treating people differently is an action toward them with a frequent unfairness condition and different argument structure.
semantic_distinction: sem_8309177d836b8a44fb585051 is grounded by the exact EN sense "tell / show the difference" and learner example "It's difficult to differentiate (distinguish) between the two varieties.". sem_aa368b4b958fdb4fe892f305 is grounded by the exact EN sense "treat differently, often unfairly" and learner example "Rules that differentiate (discriminate) between employees on the basis of gender need to be revised.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_8309177d836b8a44fb585051 means detecting or demonstrating a difference between compared items; sem_aa368b4b958fdb4fe892f305 means applying different treatment to people or things, often on an unfair basis.

reason: Far-reaching change, broadly new or major ideas, and adherence to an extreme political or social-change ideology carry different scope and domain restrictions; the political row cannot be generalized away.
semantic_distinction: sem_0b761584f57fc1f1af72b489 is grounded by the exact EN sense "basic and complete" and learner example "the need for radical (far-reaching) changes in education". sem_c06332015d8eab4585dd6526 is grounded by the exact EN sense "new and major" and learner example "radical ideas". sem_608a9152a2cd0cdd35655b3c is grounded by the exact EN sense "wanting big political change" and learner example "the radical wing of the party". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_0b761584f57fc1f1af72b489 characterizes change as basic, complete, or far-reaching; sem_c06332015d8eab4585dd6526 characterizes an idea as novel and likely to have major effects; sem_608a9152a2cd0cdd35655b3c is restricted to people, groups, or views advocating extreme political or social change.

reason: Gradual, even change over time and an unwavering or uninterrupted action impose different aspectual conditions; exports increasing and a fixed gaze are not explained by one non-circular gloss.
semantic_distinction: sem_59ac11a7ce1b109ce3fc4275 is grounded by the exact EN sense "gradually and regularly" and learner example "The company's exports have been increasing steadily.". sem_002ebd2c78f3cc15b42f977a is grounded by the exact EN sense "without changing or being interrupted" and learner example "He looked at her steadily.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_59ac11a7ce1b109ce3fc4275 describes a change progressing gradually at an even or regular rate; sem_002ebd2c78f3cc15b42f977a describes an action or state maintained without variation or interruption.

reason: The conceptual sense describes ideas or non-physical qualities, whereas the art sense describes non-representational depiction; abstract knowledge and abstract art are not substitutable uses.
semantic_distinction: sem_c9a926bec53cc127bf9c1279 is grounded by the exact EN sense "based on ideas, not physical things" and learner example "abstract knowledge/principles". sem_514c6dd903e7d8f635776190 is grounded by the exact EN sense "not showing things realistically" and learner example "abstract art". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_c9a926bec53cc127bf9c1279 concerns ideas, qualities, or knowledge lacking physical form; sem_514c6dd903e7d8f635776190 concerns visual art that deliberately does not represent people or things realistically.

reason: Combining ideas, beliefs, or styles and producing a chemical or biological substance are different processes in general and specialist domains; the retained specialist sense is not a paraphrase of the first.
semantic_distinction: sem_0a9494d8f2d472365670c001 is grounded by the exact EN sense "combination of ideas" and learner example "the synthesis of art with everyday life". sem_77d479c16633ec3423c30aeb is grounded by the exact EN sense "[specialist]production of a substance" and learner example "protein synthesis". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_0a9494d8f2d472365670c001 denotes combining ideas, influences, beliefs, or styles into a whole; sem_77d479c16633ec3423c30aeb denotes the chemical or biological production of a substance from simpler materials.

reason: A figurative duty or responsibility that causes hardship is not the physical load carried by the donkey; the metaphorical link does not make their referents or examples interchangeable.
semantic_distinction: sem_1803867b9af7b978664fda62 is grounded by the exact EN sense "duty / responsibility that causes worry / hard work" and learner example "to bear/carry/ease/reduce/share the burden". sem_102c72b93ec5c83651882699 is grounded by the exact EN sense "heavy load that is carried" and learner example "The little donkey struggled under its heavy burden.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_1803867b9af7b978664fda62 denotes a duty or responsibility that creates worry or hard work; sem_102c72b93ec5c83651882699 denotes a physically heavy load that a person or animal carries.

reason: Temporal simultaneity, contrast between propositions, and concession encode three different logical relations even though two Vietnamese connectors share a prefix.
semantic_distinction: sem_5bc79103a8b80b13b066ace3 is grounded by the exact EN sense "while" and learner example "In the UK it is illegal to drive whilst (when) holding a mobile phone.". sem_cd1f3d16e00c7b5318627755 is grounded by the exact EN sense "whereas" and learner example "Whilst my brother is hard-working, I'm quite lazy.". sem_38706299530b30f4edba28cb is grounded by the exact EN sense "although" and learner example "Whilst the results of this study are certainly interesting, they are far from conclusive.". Reviewed learner contrast: sem_5bc79103a8b80b13b066ace3 links events that occur during the same time; sem_cd1f3d16e00c7b5318627755 contrasts two propositions in the sense of whereas; sem_38706299530b30f4edba28cb concedes a fact in the sense of although.
